frame_analyses.description: Initial setup visible with 6 rubber objects - 3 green on left pegs, 3 orange on right pegs. Both graspers positioned at entry points
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Standard starting configuration for peg transfer task

frame_analyses.description: Left grasper (non-dominant hand) approaching first green object on left peg
Left grasper has secured green object and is lifting it off the peg
Green object being transferred from left grasper to right grasper in midair
Right grasper placing green object onto right-side peg
Right grasper picking up orange object from right peg for reverse direction transfer
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Beginning reverse direction sequence as required

frame_analyses.description: Orange object being transferred from right grasper to left grasper
Left grasper placing orange object onto left-side peg
Final object placements in progress, multiple objects transferred successfully
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Nearing completion of all 6 object transfers

frame_analyses.description: Task completion - all 6 objects appear to have been transferred successfully with both graspers withdrawing
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Task completed without visible dropped objects

confidence_rationale: Clear visualization of all major phase transitions and object movements. No obvious penalties observed. Completion time clearly measurable from instrument entry to final withdrawal.
cannot_determine: Exact precision of peg placement - some placements occur at edge of frame visibility
technique_summary: Strong expert-level performance in first half (forward direction) with intermediate-level performance in reverse direction. Total completion time of 72 seconds places this in intermediate range overall.
strengths: No dropped objects or fumbles observed
Proper adherence to required transfer directions
improvement_suggestions: Reduce time in reverse direction phases - particularly reverse pickup and placement
Practice maintaining expert-level speed throughout entire sequence
